eventMetadata.context_description: French President Emmanuel Macron's position remains constitutionally secure until his term ends in May 2027, despite persistent parliamentary gridlock from the 2024 snap legislative elections that produced a hung parliament and multiple prime ministerial changes, including Sébastien Lecornu's short-lived 2025 tenure. On April 24, 2026, Macron confirmed he will exit politics entirely post-term due to term limits barring a third consecutive run, signaling no early resignation intent amid approval ratings near 16%. No impeachment proceedings—requiring high treason charges and supermajorities—or snap presidential polls are viable. Government survived January 2026 no-confidence votes over budget and trade issues; upcoming 2026 budget battles and 2027 presidential primaries could heighten tensions but pose limited risk to Macron's tenure.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a slim 3% chance of the next UK general election being called by June 30, 2026, reflecting Prime Minister Keir Starmer's firm commitment to serve Labour's full term until 2029 despite mounting pressures. Recent polls from Find Out Now show Reform UK leading voting intentions at record highs while Labour hits joint lows, fueled by backlash over tax hikes, budget fallout, and policy U-turns. Today's massive local elections across England and the Welsh Senedd election represent the first major voter test since Labour's 2024 landslide, with projections of heavy losses to Reform, Lib Dems, and Greens potentially intensifying no-confidence calls or leadership challenges. Yet, traders see significant barriers to a snap vote soon, including internal party divisions and Starmer's strategy to weather the storm, absent a government collapse. Results expected overnight could shift sentiment.

title: China x India military clash by...?
description: This is a market on the likelihood of a military clash between China and India occurring by December 31.

markets.question: China x India military clash by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of China (People's Republic of China) and India between January 29, and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between Chinese and Indian military forces. Hand-to-hand combat with the use of melee weapons between military personnel, will qualify. For example the 2020 Galwan Valley clash and the 2022 Yangtse clash would both count.

Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the China Coast Guard (CCG) is part of the military, however the Indian Coast Guard (ICG) is not.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: India-China tensions along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) remain stable but fraught following 2024 patrolling pacts that enabled disengagements from standoff sites like Depsang and Demchok, ending the four-year military face-off triggered by the 2020 Galwan Valley clash. No verifiable military incidents or escalations have occurred in the past 30 days, with both armies adhering to buffer zones and coordinated patrols amid ongoing diplomatic corps commander meetings—the next slated for later in 2026. Verbal disputes persist, including a late-2025 Arunachal Pradesh spat, while mutual infrastructure builds and occasional drills signal latent rivalry. Traders monitor for patrol overlaps or external pressures that could spark friction before market resolution.

title: NATO/EU troops fighting in Ukraine by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if active military personnel officially affiliated with any NATO or EU country enter Ukraine for combat-related military purposes directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, they must be 1) officially acknowledged as active military by the NATO or EU entity or member state they are affiliated with; 2) be publicly acknowledged by NATO or an EU-affiliated entity to have entered Ukraine for a combat-related military purpose directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia.

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution they need be active duty and acknowledged as described above. Participation in a combat role is necessary for this market to resolve to "Yes" (e.g. military personnel providing training or intelligence support would not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, however drone pilots or infantry directly attacking Russian troops, or soldiers targeting and downing missiles from Ukrainian soil would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO, the EU, or member states of either entity, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **NATO maintains a policy against direct troop deployments to fight in Ukraine, emphasizing non-escalatory military aid exceeding $60 billion annually alongside EU loans to sustain Kyiv's defenses amid ongoing Russian offensives.** UK and France's January 2026 declaration of intent for post-ceasefire peacekeeping roles—envisioning coalition forces securing land, air, and sea domains—drew sharp Russian rebukes labeling foreign troops as legitimate targets, with no ceasefire materializing since. Recent US reductions in European troop presence, announced early May, intensify burden-sharing debates within the alliance but show no pivot to combat intervention. Battlefield attrition continues without signs of NATO escalation, though European rearmament targets and potential NATO summits could influence future postures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer confronts a pivotal test in today's local elections for nearly 5,000 English council seats plus devolved parliament votes in Scotland and Wales, where polls predict unprecedented Labour losses to surging Reform UK amid anti-immigration sentiment and broad disapproval (just one in seven Britons approve the government's record, with Starmer's net favorability at -48). Recent scandals, notably his disputed statements on Peter Mandelson's failed US ambassador vetting in mid-April, have sparked opposition resignation demands and whispers of Labour MPs plotting a leadership challenge, despite cabinet warnings against chaos and Starmer's pledge to fight. Results tonight could intensify internal pressures or prompt a no-confidence vote, shaping trader assessments of his tenure stability before the next general election by 2029.

title: Ukraine recognizes Russian sovereignty over its territory by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a deal in which Ukraine agrees to formally recognize Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory is reached between the Russian Federation and Ukraine by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The deal must be a publicly announced mutual agreement. 

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995, “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia, which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement, is an example of a qualifying agreement.

An official unilateral pledge by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution even if not part of an agreement with Russia.

The April 19, 2013, Brussels Agreement between Serbia and Kosovo, in which Serbia recognized Kosovo’s de facto administration but did not formally grant de jure recognition, would not be considered a qualifying agreement under this market, as this market requires formal recognition of sovereignty rather than acknowledgment of administrative control.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by Ukraine and/or the Russian Federation, however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military chief Kyrylo Budanov have repeatedly affirmed that Ukraine will never recognize Russian sovereignty over Crimea, Donbas, or other occupied territories, citing legal prohibitions, public opposition, and the principle of non-capitulation to invasion. US-mediated peace talks in Geneva in February 2026 and prior rounds in Abu Dhabi stalled over Russia's demands for territorial concessions, with no breakthroughs since. Ongoing escalations, including Russian airstrikes killing dozens on May 5 and Ukrainian deep strikes into Russia, underscore active hostilities without diplomatic progress. Upcoming Russian unilateral ceasefires around May 8-9 offer slim prospects for de-escalation, but trader consensus reflects Ukraine's firm stance barring any recognition absent major shifts.

This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects razor-thin uncertainty in Harvey Weinstein's post-MeToo legal saga, pitting No Prison Time at 32.4% against 20-30 years at 31.2%, driven by his third New York rape retrial underway since mid-April 2026. Key accuser Jessica Mann wrapped five days of testimony last week in the case alleging 2013 third-degree rape—previously deadlocked in 2025—potentially adding up to 25 years consecutive to his upheld 16-year California rape sentence from 2022, whose appeal drew a skeptical panel last month. Weinstein, 74 and wheelchair-bound at Rikers amid health woes including heart issues, fuels release bets via possible mistrial, compassionate options, or slim appeal odds, while prosecutors leverage industry reckoning for conviction momentum; verdict looms as the pivotal swing factor.

title: Will OpenAI launch a consumer hardware product by...?
description: This is a market on whether OpenAI will release a consumer hardware product by the specified date.

markets.question: Will OpenAI launch a new consumer hardware product in 2025?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI publicly announces and launches a new consumer hardware product by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

A "consumer hardware product" is defined as a physical device intended for direct use by individual consumers, rather than enterprise or developer tools. The product must be newly introduced and not a rebrand, update, or iteration of any previously released device.

Examples that would qualify include an AI-powered wearable, smart home assistant, or augmented reality device. Developer tools, AI chips, or servers marketed exclusively for enterprise use would not qualify.

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent supply chain leaks from analyst Ming-Chi Kuo in late April 2026 reveal OpenAI accelerating development of an AI-agent-first smartphone, featuring custom MediaTek Dimensity chips on TSMC's N2P process, dual NPUs for on-device processing, and Luxshare manufacturing, with mass production eyed for H1 2027 and 30 million units shipped over two years. This builds on the Jony Ive collaboration for screenless, context-aware devices like potential earbuds codenamed "Dime," though earlier H2 2026 unveil plans slipped to 2027 per court filings. No consumer hardware has shipped yet, underscoring execution risks for OpenAI's software-to-hardware pivot amid rivalry with Apple's Siri ecosystem and Google's Gemini devices. Traders should monitor developer conferences and official statements for launch signals.

title: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by...?
description: This is a market on the likelihood of Russia capturing Kostyantynivka by a specific date.

markets.question: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by August 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Kostyantynivka railroad station located on Pravoberezhna vulytsia by August 31, 2025 at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by August 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Train Station Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka3.jpg

Train Station Location in Kostyantynivka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka2.jpg

Kostyantynivka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka1.jpg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/hZ6ch5jVRvkNFY317

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue incremental advances toward Kostyantynivka, a key fortress city in Donetsk Oblast's defensive belt, with intense clashes on its southern and eastern outskirts driving trader sentiment. As of May 6, ISW reported milblogger claims of Russian progress in western Kostyantynivka, following Defense Ministry assertions of capturing nearby Novodmytrivka on May 2 and earlier gains like Illinivka in late April. Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi confirmed repelling multiple infiltration attempts, maintaining positions amid 19 attacks in the sector last reported. Over the past 30 days, Russian prioritization of Donetsk has yielded tactical improvements but stalled full encirclement, with Ukrainian counteractions and unmanned ground vehicle deployments slowing momentum. Upcoming weather shifts and reinforcement flows could tip the frontline balance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez ruled out snap elections for Spain's Cortes Generales on April 29, 2026, affirming plans to govern until 2027 per constitutional timelines and beyond with progressive policies amid ongoing minority government challenges. Opposition leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo of the Popular Party (PP) continues pressing for early polls, citing PSOE corruption scandals, judicial investigations, and recent regional defeats for Sánchez's Socialists, including snap votes in Aragon where PP-Vox coalitions strengthened in February-March. Public opinion polls indicate over 60% favor elecciones anticipadas, fueled by coalition fragility with parties like Junts, but no-confidence motions lack sufficient support. Traders weigh Sánchez's firm stance against mounting parliamentary pressures, with budget negotiations and regional dynamics as key near-term catalysts.

title: US x Russia military clash by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Russia between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between U.S. and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify. 

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage. 

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid ongoing Russian strikes on Ukrainian cities that killed over 20 on May 4, 2026, both Russia and Ukraine declared competing unilateral ceasefires around Victory Day, with Russia extending its pause but threatening a massive missile barrage on Ukraine's center. These de-escalation gestures highlight mutual restraint in the four-year proxy war, where US military aid to Kyiv—including long-range weapons—has drawn Moscow's warnings of NATO "at war" status without prompting direct confrontation. Reestablished US-Russia military dialogue since February 2026 and nuclear deterrence remain key barriers to escalation, though US intelligence flags rising risks from space-based threats and stalled talks. Traders eye upcoming negotiations or battlefield shifts for potential volatility.

title: Will Russia invade a NATO country by...?

eventMetadata.context_description: European intelligence agencies, including Dutch MIVD, warn Russia could prepare for NATO conflict within a year of ending its Ukraine invasion, potentially targeting Baltic states to secure Kaliningrad access via the Suwałki Gap. Polish PM Tusk recently cautioned of attacks within months amid hybrid escalations like sabotage, drone incursions, and cyberattacks on Poland and Baltics. NATO bolsters eastern flank defenses through exercises and troop rotations, while Swedish officials highlight risks to islands like Gotland. No direct military incursions reported, but stalled Ukraine ceasefire talks and Russian laws enabling "protective" deployments abroad fuel trader concerns over Article 5 tests before 2027 summits.

title: Trump eliminates capital gains tax on crypto by ___?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump signs a bill into law or performs any executive action changing US tax law to eliminate capital gains tax on any cryptocurrency assets by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Such a bill must apply to US taxpaying persons in general; if a law applies only to specific companies or institutions, it will not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market.

If capital gains is eliminated only for specific types or classes of crypto assets (e.g., only Bitcoin or Ethereum, only American issued cryptocurrencies, or only NFTs), it will still count toward a "Yes" resolution for this market.

If the bill is signed into law, or executive action is instituted before the resolution date, it will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of when it takes effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US federal government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the fall of Bashar al-Assad in December 2024, Syria's interim President Ahmed al-Sharaa has pursued diplomatic engagement with Israel, emphasizing seriousness about a security agreement to stabilize the border and end Israeli strikes. Recent statements from al-Sharaa in mid-April 2026 affirmed that negotiations are "not at a dead end," despite earlier claims in late March that Israel backed out of broader normalization at the last minute over territorial disputes, including a buffer zone near the Golan Heights that Israel seized post-Assad and refuses to relinquish without guarantees. US envoy Tom Barrack expressed optimism on April 17 for quicker non-aggression pacts with Syria than Lebanon, reflecting Damascus's conciliatory posture amid reconstruction needs. No formal normalization has occurred, with sovereignty red lines and Israeli security demands as key hurdles; upcoming US-brokered talks could advance de-escalation or stall further.

These incremental gains, averaging small square kilometers daily, reflect slowed momentum in Russia's Spring-Summer 2026 campaign, as Ukrainian forces conduct strikes and reposition to prepared lines, preventing breakthroughs toward Sumy City's railroad station—the market's resolution criterion—still dozens of kilometers from the frontline. Ongoing positional combat and unit redeployments could influence future escalation, but no major diplomatic or military catalysts loom immediately.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes lead Polymarket's 2026 Stanley Cup trader consensus after both swept their first-round playoff matchups last week, earning rest advantages ahead of second-round action that began Saturday with Carolina's 3-0 shutout over Philadelphia. The Avalanche, Presidents' Trophy winners and top Western seed, boast Nathan MacKinnon's MVP-caliber form and a deep blue line, while the Hurricanes' elite penalty kill and Frederik Andersen's goaltending anchor their Metropolitan Division dominance, keeping the top probabilities tightly bunched at 38.4% and 31.5%. Vegas Golden Knights and surging Buffalo Sabres trail closely on strong regular-season finishes and early playoff momentum, with minimal star injuries heightening upset potential in a wide-open bracket.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With qualifiers complete in early April—capping UEFA playoffs where Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Sweden, and Türkiye advanced—the 48-team field is set for the expanded FIFA World Cup, driving tightly bunched trader consensus atop Polymarket as no squad dominates amid group stage leniency (top two plus eight best thirds advance). France's 17% implied probability leads on elite depth, Kylian Mbappé's scoring form through qualifiers, and Nations League resilience, narrowly ahead of Spain's 15% fueled by Euro 2024 triumph, Lamine Yamal's wizardry, and fluid attack. England (11%) boasts Premier League stars like Jude Bellingham but falters on conversion; Argentina and Brazil (9% each) leverage Lionel Messi's nous and South American grit yet grapple with aging cores versus revitalized rivals in a knockout-volatility tournament across North America.
